sprint_progression.note: Headline flat (+0.5 pp) but masks two significant wins: in05 +13.5 pp (spread injection breakthrough) and in09 +7.5 pp. Both masked by in13 parse regression (-13.0 pp, 24/40 parseable) and in10 variance (-7.1 pp). True signal positive.

in05_spread_injection_breakthrough.mechanism: Survey-prompt spread injection broke the 100% A lock. Opposition/moderate personas now choosing C (staying the same). 62.5% A vs Pew 68% A — near target. Remaining gap: B=0% vs Pew 13% (no 'somewhat stronger' responses). Note: spread note says BJP=stronger, opposition=same/weaker — binary split, no middle ground.

in09_spread_partial.mechanism: Spread injection produced first C responses (5% vs Pew 7%) and tiny A movement (2.5%). B dropped from 100% to 92.5%. Direction correct but A still 2.5% vs Pew 41% — BJP 'a lot' trust not firing.

in02_narrative_constraint_partial.mechanism: Narrative constraint partially worked — B fell from 97.4% to 89.5%, C rose from 2.6% to 10.5%. Model is less lock-stepped. BUT A still 0%. Root cause NOT fully resolved: economic context also bleeds through budget_ceiling (ImmutableConstraints), tendency_summary (financial carefulness language), and life_defining_events. Narrative text alone was not the only channel.

multi_channel_economic_contamination.finding: Economic hardship bleeds into BJP political answers through FOUR memory channels, not just narrative text. Blocking the narrative (A-6 fix) addressed only one.

channels.1_narrative_text: Fixed in A-6 — BJP narrative constraint added to narrative_generator.py
channels.2_budget_ceiling: STILL ACTIVE — lower-income BJP personas have budget_ceiling like 'Rs 15,000-20,000/month — careful with every rupee'. Injected in decide.py as 'Budget reality: ...' This appears in the system prompt when answering political questions.
channels.3_tendency_summary: STILL ACTIVE — BehaviouralTendencies.reasoning_prompt for lower-income personas includes financial caution language that bleeds into political hedging.
channels.4_life_defining_events: STILL ACTIVE — Life stories can include economic hardship events even if the narrative text doesn't mention them.

multi_channel_economic_contamination.evidence: A-6 in02 sample: 'I can't give very favorable when my kitchen budget tells a different story every month.' This persona was regenerated with the narrative constraint — kitchen budget comes from budget_ceiling or tendency_summary, not the narrative.
multi_channel_economic_contamination.fix_a7: Gate budget_ceiling injection for India BJP personas in decide.py — suppress 'Budget reality:' line for bjp_supporter/bjp_lean when answering surveys. The budget constraint is irrelevant to political approval questions.

in15_climate_lived_experience_override.finding: Climate spread injection (in_SPREAD_QUESTION_NOTES) not added for in15 in A-6. Also, even if added, flood/drought life events in persona narratives are likely to override BJP climate stance. In15 still 100% A.
in15_climate_lived_experience_override.fix_a7: Add in15 to _SPREAD_QUESTION_NOTES in simulatte_runner.py with explicit BJP 'somewhat of a threat' anchor. Also check if the bjp_supporter policy_stance climate anchor is reaching the decide prompt.

in13_parse_degradation.finding: Parse rate: 24/40 in A-6 vs 29/40 in A-5. The in13 question produces verbose responses ('Somewhat disagree. I understand why...') that fail the single-letter parse. The option-text prefix parser (metrics.py rule 2b) should catch 'Somewhat disagree' but isn't being triggered.
in13_parse_degradation.fix_a7: Add in13 to _SPREAD_QUESTION_NOTES with explicit instruction: 'You MUST respond with ONLY the single letter A, B, C or D. Nothing else.' Reinforce the parse requirement specifically for this question.

in04_inc_modal_C_lock.finding: Two-field INC anchoring (current_conditions_stance + policy_stance) failed — still 90% C, 0% A, 0% D. Sample responses show ALL personas hedging to C ('somewhat unfavorable') regardless of lean. The 'pragmatic moderate' framing dominates even for bjp_supporter and opposition personas.
in04_inc_modal_C_lock.evidence: in04 A-6 sample: 'I won't say very unfavorable because I'm moderate and pragmatic' — this is a bjp_supporter persona who has the 'VERY unfavorable — option D' anchor in TWO fields but still hedges to C.
in04_inc_modal_C_lock.fix_a7: The 'pragmatic moderate' tendency in the persona's behavioural_tendencies is overriding the political stance. Fix: add INC anchor to the BJP persona's IDENTITY STATEMENT level — the first 25 words that become the identity_statement. This is the highest-priority injection point in the decide prompt.

in07.note: RLHF hard ceiling. No change. A=0%, B=0%, C=92.1%, D=7.9%.

in12.note: RLHF hard ceiling. No change. A=0%, B=30.6%, C=69.4%.

in13.note: Parse regression (24/40). Distribution direction unchanged. RLHF ceiling confirmed at A=0%.

sprint_roadmap.A-7_priority_1: Gate budget_ceiling injection for India BJP personas in decide.py. In _decide_core_memory_block(), skip the 'Budget reality: ...' line when persona is bjp_supporter or bjp_lean. This is the main remaining economic contamination channel into political approval answers (in02/in08/in09).
sprint_roadmap.A-7_priority_2: Add in15 to _SPREAD_QUESTION_NOTES in simulatte_runner.py — same pattern as in05. Explicit BJP anchor: 'your answer is somewhat of a threat, not major'.
sprint_roadmap.A-7_priority_3: Add in13 to _SPREAD_QUESTION_NOTES with parse reinforcement: 'You MUST respond with ONLY the single letter A, B, C or D. Nothing else.' Parse rate 24/40 unacceptable.
sprint_roadmap.A-7_priority_4: in09 BJP A-trust: strengthen spread note for in09 — current note got C responses but not A. Add more explicit: 'If you are a BJP supporter, your answer is A — a lot — not B, not somewhat, but A LOT.'
sprint_roadmap.A-7_priority_5: in04 INC modal-C lock: add INC stance to BJP persona NARRATIVE IDENTITY at generation time — if bjp_supporter, narrative must include 'deeply critical of Congress/INC'. This is the remaining injection point that hasn't been tried.
sprint_roadmap.A-7_track_2: Sarvam evaluation for in07/in12/in13 — RLHF hard ceiling confirmed across 6 sprints.
